content.full_text:
August 18, 1992


FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

To whom it may concern:

The question is very simple. Councilwomens (newly elected) Daughter, who
is divorced and has two children and is employed by the City of Tehachapi
where she serves on the Council. (Clerk)

Is there a conflict of interest when the Councilwomen votes on wages and
benefits on a recently completed budget?

City Attorney ruled that there was none.

In a recent League of California Cities meeting, I was told that this "was
a conflict" of interest on the Councilwomans part.

Thank you much for a speedy reply.

Our Ben Madlock type Attornery has been with the City for about twenty seven
years and is about to retire.


Many thanks;

Alfred M. Damian
City Councilman
City of Tehachapi
Tehachapi, CA., 93561

cc: file


FPPC

AUG24 1992

RECEIVED

[The image appears to be mostly blank/white with very faint text that is largely illegible. At the bottom of the page, there appears to be some handwritten text that reads:]

SCC # & SHA

[The rest of the document appears to be blank or too faint to read with certainty]
